The output also notes in the introductory summary that the Form ADV disclosures were made 'through Whitmore and Chao,' directly implicating Whitmore in the Form ADV misrepresentation allegation.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly mentions that the Hargrove & Tilton compliance review 'did not conduct statistical testing of trade allocations, did not compare profitable allocation rates across accounts, and did not identify the allocation-related deficiencies that subsequently emerged' in the 'Inadequate Personal Trading Account Monitoring and Reporting' section. criteria_results.title: Key Dates: Examination Timeline Accurately Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not accurately state the key examination timeline dates. The output repeatedly uses placeholder text like '11' and '22' instead of actual dates (e.g., 'The SEC examination had commenced on 11,' 'issued a deficiency letter on 11,' 'On or about 22'). The only specific date correctly stated is the referral date of October 18, 2024. The examination commencement date (March 11, 2024), deficiency letter date (August 2, 2024), referral notice date (November 6, 2024), and service date (November 12, 2024) are not correctly stated - they appear as unfilled placeholders. Since fewer than three of the five required dates are correctly stated, this fails the criterion.

criteria_results.title: Email Exhibit Language Quoted or Paraphrased
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does reference the Whitmore-to-Delgado email and quotes its language ('clean up the shared drive' and 'clean up of legacy files on the shared drive'). However, the criterion requires that the output note the email's ambiguous language as 'potentially supporting an innocent interpretation.' The agent's output does the opposite — it characterizes the email as corroborating obstructive intent and uses the language to support the allegation of obstruction, never acknowledging that the phrasing could support an innocent interpretation. Since the criterion specifically requires noting the ambiguous language as potentially supporting an innocent interpretation, and the agent fails to do this, the criterion is not satisfied.
